attacking_style: Counterattacks from breakaways with 10-15km remaining, particularly on final climbs. Waits for others to commit before launching winning move on uphill finishes.
team_tactics: Benefits significantly from family/team cooperation—brother Gorka's positioning in 2020 Vuelta exemplifies tactical coordination. Performs best when protected rather than working as domestique, as shown at Paris-Nice podium.
terrain_preference: Final climbs from breakaways where climbing superiority over specialists decides the outcome. Descents into technical finishes (Nice descent counterattack). Vulnerable in bunch sprints from small groups despite having competitive speed.
vulnerability: Hesitates to initiate sprints from small groups despite possessing speed—froze in Tour de France 8-rider sprint. Positioning lapses in crosswind stages (2017 crash). Needs others to chase before counterattacking.
key_patterns: Counterattacks from breakaways at 10-15km to go, not initial aggressor (2019, 2020 Vuelta wins)
Superior climbing from breakaway groups allows late separation from specialists (2020 Vuelta, 2019 Paris-Nice)
GC podium capable when protected by team, not as sole leader (3rd Paris-Nice 2021, 2018)
Sprint hesitation from small groups despite speed—waits too long to commit (2018 TDF)
Benefits from brother/teammate positioning creating tactical advantages (2020 Vuelta stage win setup)
form_trajectory: positive: 2021 Paris-Nice podium shows continued GC capability when protected, building on stage wins in 2019-2020
